answer:
Since the speed of the raft downstream is directly proportional to the speed of the current, and the speed of the current is the same in both directions, the speed of the raft downstream is the average of the speeds downstream and upstream. The time it takes for the raft to float downstream is the reciprocal of the speed downstream.

The average speed is 5.833333333333333, so the time it takes for the raft to float downstream is 1 / 5.833333333333333 = 0.17142857142857142.
